SPECIAL NOTE: There are some things in this report that might ruffle a lot of
feathers. That’s OK, I recently had something of a heart-to-heart with a bunch of
my readers, and it really got me fired up and I decided that it was time that
SOMEBODY spilled the beans…
“What They Aren’t Telling You…
The 19 Marketing and Copywriting
Secrets That Will Solve Nearly Any
Business Problem You Have”
The “Copy That Sells” Report
Version 1.1
October 11th
From the desk of Bill Glazer
Glazer-Kennedy Inner Circle
I have 19 copywriting secrets I am going to share with you…
but first I have to tell you exactly WHY I am have prepared
this report for you.
Because, to be frank, I am not usually in the business of giving
away my secrets… in fact, my clients regularly pay me tens of
thousands of dollars for my coaching and consulting and home
study courses.
However, I decided to make an exception this time. Keep
reading to find out why…
I am going to be blunt here, because I am more than a little bit ticked off…
There is a lot of mis-information out there about copywriting. I know, because I just
asked a couple thousand people about it… and boy did I get an earful!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 19 --

They told me what their problems are. What their questions are. And they told me exactly
what was holding them back in their business.
And the amazing thing is – even though you have probably been told otherwise, the
solution is just not very complicated.
Which is GOOD - I like to keep things simple and to the point. I like to find a formula
that works, and I like to apply that formula over and over. I figure if I find something
that’s working, I just need to find a way to use that formula more often.
But That’s Not What You’ve Been Taught…
This is the part that makes me mad… when I surveyed those thousands of people about
their marketing and copywriting, I kept hearing a few common refrain:
“My copy will never be as good as Guru X or Guru Y”
Of course, they didn’t really say “Guru X” or “Guru Y”. They gave me an actual name –
but I went ahead and changed the name to protect the guilty.
That’s what really makes me mad. There are people running around that are trying to
make all of this look a lot harder than it is. There are various reasons for this, but the
bottom line is that they all have a vested interest in making copywriting look difficult.
“About Those Mystic-Gurus…”
If you have studied marketing and copywriting at all, you probably know what I
mean when I say “Mystic-Guru.” These are the folks who do their best to create
this impression that you need to have some “black-magic” skills to write good
copy.
They would have you believe that they are like some modern-day alchemists that
turn lead into gold. But instead they are using ink on paper, or pixels on a
computer screen.
Well, here’s the thing – they are sorta half-right.
Because once you learn how to write good sales copy, then you literally can sit
down, write some words out, and then watch those words turn into big-time cash.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 19 --

You can literally turn words into money.
But here is the LIE…
You don’t have to be a super-human to get great results with your copy. You
don’t need to be a “Mystic-Guru.” You don’t have to go on a trek to some distant
mountain top and worship some arcane copywriting gods.
This isn’t some crazy magic trick.
I know it may SOUND like magic that you could sit down and write an ad or a
sales letter and then have mountains of cash come flying into your business… but
there isn’t any magic involved.
And yes, it sounds like magic when you can keep EVERYTHING in your
business EXACTLY the same, but write a new or better sales letter and DOUBLE
your bottom line.
Sure – it sounds like magic… and those Mystic-Gurus would love you to believe
that it’s magic (and, of course, they are the only ones that know the magic
spell)… but the reality is that copywriting is just a skill – you just need to learn
how to do it, and then apply it correctly.
If I Can Do It…
I make a not-so-small fortune from my copywriting… both creating copy for my
own business as well as writing copy for clients.
And I have personally written 19 different sales letters that each generated more
than $1 million in sales.
HOWEVER, it hasn’t always been this way for me. And that’s why I know this is
a skill that YOU CAN MASTER.
I know this… because until about 10 years ago I had never heard of direct
marketing or copyrighting. I first heard about it when I attended a seminar and
heard Dan Kennedy speak. It was like a huge light bulb went off in my head… I
was instantly hooked. And I set myself to the task of learning everything I could
about direct marketing and copywriting.
Now I never considered myself a writer before. I mean, we all write stuff in
school, but I sure wasn’t some Ernest Hemingway.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 19 --

But I have always been a bit obsessive when I go about learning things… and this
was right up my alley – I poured myself into studying copywriting, and I got good
at it – very good.
I don’t tell you that to brag… the reason I tell you this is because I am about to
break all the rules with this report – and I just want you to understand that I know
what I am talking about…
OK, First The Good News…
OK, first things first – there is a LOT of money in copywriting.
I am talking about an INSANE amount of money. More money than you can
possibly imagine.
Good copy has the power to completely transform a business. If a business is just
starting out, it can put that business on the map.
If a business is slowly dying on the vine, good copy can turn that business around.
If you are having trouble raising capital – guess what, good copy is your answer.
Are competitors giving you problems? Well, you know the answer – you are just
one sales letter away from leaving your competitors in your dust.
It goes on and on. In fact, I like to say that the answer to every business
problem is GOOD COPY.
So when you start to think about putting a few extra percent (or more) onto the
bottom line of just about any business in the WORLD… well you can start to
understand when I tell you that we are talking about an INSANE amount of
money.
If you have that power – the power to write copy that sells, well you can start to
understand just how powerful that position is.
Watch Out For The “Mystic-Gurus”… And The Posers!
With all that money riding on good copy, well is it any wonder that there is a huge
mystique around it? And a HUGE amount of mis-information?
This is the deal – there is a saying that I learned from Dan Kennedy… he says:
“Beware The Wizard.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 19 --

By that, Dan means that you really have to watch out WHO you learn from. If you
are going to spend your time and money to learn a business skill, you have to
make sure that the person you are learning from is teaching from EXPERIENCE.
You also have to make sure that they actually have the skill to teach what they do.
There are folks who write great copy, but they don’t have a clue how to share that
skill in a way that anyone can understand.
Even worse are those wizards who conspire to keep you begging for table scraps.
These are the Mystic-Gurus who would have you believe that copywriting is
black-magic.
Believe me, the results might LOOK like magic – but copywriting is a skill, and
it’s a skill that you can learn.
In fact, it’s…
The One Skill That You Absolutely Need…
Here is the thing – no matter where you are in your business, copywriting is the one skill
that you absolutely need.
And it’s the one skill that you really shouldn’t outsource.
If you are just starting out – maybe you are a one-person shop – then you NEED to be
able to write copy. You probably can’t afford a top-flight copywriter. And even if you
can afford to get your copy written by someone else, you will still need to know enough
to evaluate that copy.
On the other hand, maybe you have a larger business with a staff. You know the only
way to leverage is to outsource almost everything to employees and contractors.
HOWEVER, you can’t outsource your marketing and your copy.
I mean, you can have someone WRITE your copy – but you need to have enough
copywriting skills to be able to hire the right person and evaluate that copy. This isn’t like
getting someone to run your copier.
I’ve Been There Too…
No matter what your copywriting skills, I have been there too…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 19 --

Like I said above, ten years ago I had never heard of direct marketing. So if you are a
newbie with copywriting, I can vividly remember what it was like… all very confusing at
first.
But then I remember the incredible feeling of power when I wrote my first piece of
copy… and it started to pull RESULTS!
It was like I had the keys to the kingdom at last.
I had just learned a skill that enabled me to create cash-flow on demand… whether I was
promoting my product or someone else’s product.
You can learn this skill just as easily as I did. And you can feel that same incredible
power when you learn how to write words that get people to send you money
Street Cred… Those 19 Different “Million
Dollar” Letters That I Wrote
Of course, if I had stayed there, at that newbie beginner level, then you wouldn’t be
reading this report right now…
But I didn’t – I kept at it, working at copy and studying… and now I have more people
approaching me to write copy than I could ever help. And they are paying me very large
sums of money when I can fit them into my schedule.
Now you might think this is a remarkable story – to go from beginner to top of the heap
in a relatively short period of time… but that’s really not what this report is about.
My goal here is to convince you that copywriting is something that YOU need, and it’s
something that is well within your grasp.
And I am going to do that by sharing a bunch of killer copywriting SECRETS that are
going to give you the jump start that you need…
Here We Go… The 19 Copywriting Secrets
You Haven’t Been Told…
Secret #1: Your copy doesn’t need to be perfect
This is what people told me in my survey… they are worried because they don’t
know if there copy is good enough.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 19 --

Or they don’t know WHEN it’s good enough.
Well let me clue you in – no writing is ever perfect.
If you put some of the world’s best copywriters into a group and ask them to
review a headline, they will keep on working on that headline until you take it
away from them – whether it’s in 10 minutes or 10 hours.
I know this because I have actually seen that exact scenario.
After about five minutes they will have a very good headline. Then they will
tweak it and tweak it… and somewhere around a half hour they will accidentally
tweak it right back to that original headline they had after 10 minutes. 
This is the point – you need to stop worrying about getting your copy perfect. You
just need to get the building blocks in place, and then your copy will sell.
I will cover this a bit more in some of the other secrets, but the reality is that if
you follow the right formula and the secrets I have outlined below, your copy will
be “good enough” to pull serious results.
Secret #2: The copywriting formula
This is a HUGE secret – one that ALL of the top copywriters use.
They don’t reinvent the wheel. They have a basic formula, and they use it over
and over. Sometimes the formula varies a bit… but once you know the formula,
you can use it over and over and over.
The sales letters all look different, because they are for different products.
And different copywriters eventually develop their own slightly tweaked version
of the formula. But they all use it.
I have actually taken this and further refined it down to 11 Building Blocks that
are mandatory to any piece of copy. That’s what I use in my own copy.
But the bottom line is that all good professional copywriters keep going back to
the same well… and they will keep on doing that until the well goes dry.
And you know what, that well never will run dry… because good copy just keeps
on working.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 19 --

This is the deal: the person reading your copy – the prospect – doesn’t care
whether you spent a month on your sales letter or whether you spent a couple of
hours.
They don’t care and they don’t know that you just pulled out your formula and
cranked out that letter with almost no effort.
If the formula works, you just stick with it.
Now I can’t possibly give you my complete formula in this report. But believe
me, I wouldn’t consider sitting down to write any copy without having my
formula in front of me.
And I am not alone… every top copywriter does this – in fact, this is how you get
your copywriting done much faster and more efficiently. I won’t go so far as to
say it makes writing copy effortless, but it sure makes it a lot easier.
Secret #3: You don’t need to be a guru
Let me say this right now… it really isn’t your fault. You have heard these
amazing tales from some copywriting superheroes about how they sweated blood
over their amazing sales letters (sooner or later these stories take on a tale of their
own – soon we are going to hear that they were actually WRITTEN in blood!)
And you undoubtedly have seen examples of some of this amazing copy. In fact,
some of this copy has been just about shoved down your throat.
But guess what?
In your market, you probably don’t need that amazing copy. If your copy is 80%
as good as the super-guru copy, then you are going to clean up… and CRUSH
your competition.
And let me tell you – it isn’t very hard to get your copy 80% as good as that
super-hero copy.
It all goes back to Secret #1 and Secret #2 – your copy doesn’t need to be perfect
– you just need to follow a basic copywriting formula. You don’t need to turn
your copy into art. You just need it to sell.
ONE MORE KEY PIECE: good is good enough! So many times I have coaching
and consulting clients that keep going over a piece of copy time after time after
time. They keep tweaking it and changing it and working on it. But they never get
it out in the market.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 19 --

And I will tell you this, if you never finish your copy and publish it, you will
NEVER make any sales. It is better to get a piece that is 80% right out into the
market, than have a piece that is 99% right that you never publish. Good is good
enough!
Secret #4: Spend Your Time On The Headline
This is the secret that shouldn’t be a secret – your headline is the single most
important piece of your copy. If you blow it here, then the rest of your copy can
be pure gold and IT WON’T MATTER.
Because if your headline doesn’t work, then no one will ever see the rest of your
copy.
So you need to spend a larger proportion of your time on the headline. Get this
one piece right, and the rest of the copy falls into place.
One of the things I always do is write a whole bunch of headlines. Seriously,
spend your time here – that time is well spent… because you will end up with a
great headline. And here is an advanced tip - the headlines that you DON’T use
will become sub-headlines.
So please be clear on this: I sometimes spend as much time on the headline as I do
on the entire rest of the letter. You need a good headline.
Secret #5: Don’t Spend Too Much Time On Your
Headline
OK, now this is the secret that really turned things around for me…
In Secret #4 I told you about the power of the headline, and how it’s the most
important piece of any copy.
And I told you how this is where you need to spend a lot of your time and effort if
you are going to create effective copy.
But here is the kicker – you don’t need to reinvent the wheel. There are LOTS of
great headlines that have ALREADY BEEN WRITTEN.
Most of the time, you can simply RECYCLE a headline. Of course, you will have
to tweak it a bit for your product and your market… but you don’t have to start
from scratch.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 19 --

Other people have slaved over headlines and tested them forever. Why not go
with what has already been proven?
When I realized I could do this (actually, when I realized that this was what the
top copywriters in the world did), well that is when everything changed for me.
All of a sudden, creating great copy got a lot easier… and the quality of my copy
got a lot better.
And one other thing… I started building a headline “swipe file.” And that swipe
file became one of my most prized possessions.
In fact, if you took everything away from me, I could start over very quickly with
just my copywriting skills. But when you took everything, I would be a lot
happier if you left my headline swipe file. 
Secret #6: Copy Is Copy
Once you learn to create good copy (and like I said above, it’s a lot simpler than
you think), then you will discover something… and this is it: good copy is good
copy, no matter what format.
And once this fact sinks in, you will start to use that basic copywriting formula in
just about everything you do.
When you create a yellow pages ad, when you write an email to your current
clients, when you write a press release, when you apply for a loan at the bank,
when you put a subject line in an email… it is all copy. Even when you are giving
a talk, or you are running a meeting, or you are writing a review.
And all of a sudden your ability to INFLUENCE people will skyrocket.
This is what’s going on… copywriting is all about influencing action. And once
you understand the basic underlying principles (the underlying formula), then you
have the keys to the kingdom.
Secret #7: Long copy vs. short copy
This is one that people like to get worked up about… sorta the way that some
people like to argue about politics or religion.
The big difference is that, unlike politics and religion, this one really DOES have
an answer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 19 --

Here is the answer: the best copy is the copy that GETS RESULTS.
In general, longer copy tends to get better results - but the real answer is that your
copy can’t be too long, it can only be too boring.
If you have a story to tell, and you tell that story well… then your prospects will
read your copy. If they aren’t reading your copy, then they either aren’t good
prospects or you are boring them.
Amazingly enough – getting them hooked into your copy all goes back to
following the formula (that’s Secret #1). In fact, I have a specific formula that I
use just for the opening paragraph in a sales letter – the idea is you grab the reader
by the throat immediately, and then you don’t let go.
Again, I can’t give you every detail of my formula here… but the key piece for
you to understand is that this isn’t some black magic that the super-hero
copywriters conjure up with some Jedi mind trick. This stuff is just another skill
that anyone can learn.
Secret #8: How to know what to change in your copy
Lots of times people will get stuck when their copy isn’t working – they don’t
know what to change.
Well… I obviously can’t give you the answer for YOUR specific situation in this
report, but there is always one place to start when it’s time to tweak your copy –
and that place is your headline.
As we covered in Secret #4, the headline is the single most important part of your
copy.
So if your copy isn’t working, this is the place to start with your changes. It’s
really that simple. After you work on your headline, then you can go on to other
things, like your offer or your bonuses or your price. But start with the headline.
And like I said above, the easiest way to have great headlines is to have a great
headline swipe file.
AND REMEMBER: good is good enough!
Secret #9: Good Copywriting Will Work In Your Market
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 19 --

If I had a dime for every time that someone has told me “that copy works great in
your market, but the people in my market won’t read that stuff”, well then I could
probably buy my own island in the Caribbean.
Seriously… this seems to be a disease. Everyone thinks their market is
“different.”
They say “that stuff only works on consumers, it doesn’t work for business to
business products.’
Or they say “I wish my market was business to business so I could use that stuff,
but I’m selling to consumers and they won’t read it.”
These people are just plain blind to this one simple fact – people are people, and
they all make decisions the same way.
And for the majority of people, they make those decisions based on certain
underlying principles that have not changed for thousands of years.
People buy because of emotions. They buy what they want, not what they need.
They are more influenced by a fear of loss than a potential for gain. They buy
solutions, not features.
The people in your market respond to those same things, whether you are selling
to consumers or business people. Whether you have a non-profit or you are selling
to institutions. Whether your product is high-priced or low-priced.
The bottom line is that good copy sells no matter what the market, and no matter
what the product.
Secret #10: Direct Mail Copy Is Copy
I cut my teeth writing copy for direct mail. Long form sales letters sent via the
mail and Fed Ex, postcards, self mailers. I have done it all.
And guess what – that copywriting formula I mentioned earlier… it works for all
of it.
Maybe your business is 100% online. Maybe your business is 100% offline. It
doesn’t matter, it all applies.
Of course, there are specific differences. Believe me, I have already made all the
mistakes… you have to know the right formats (and the INEXPENSIVE formats),
and there are some mechanics you have to get right.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 19 --

But the bottom line is that if you learn to write copy that sells, then you will do
just fine via direct mail.
KEY POINT: if your business is 100% ONLINE… well then you are literally
leaving FREE MONEY on the table. Adding an offline direct mail component
into your business is “easy as pie” and almost always will increase your profits in
a very big way.
The problem is that a lot of people get intimidated by direct mail. But remember,
good copy is good copy.
Secret #11: The reader’s path
This is one that even some of the “experts” miss… your readers will take different
paths through your sales letter.
In fact, they will have an almost infinite number of paths through your copy. But
there are TWO PATHS that your copy ABSOLUTELY must be written for…
The Linear Path and the Skimmers Path.
Some of your prospects will read the entire sales letter straight through from top
to bottom. This is the easy one that most people write for.
However, many copywriters (even some of the gurus) completely forget about the
Skimmers… and depending on your market and your product, the Skimmers
might represent the MAJORITY of your readers.
The Skimmers will glance over your copy, quickly moving through various
sections. Sometimes they will immediately go to the end and read the PS, and
then go back and breeze through the letter.
You NEED to write for both the Linear readers and the Skimmers. If you don’t, you will
kill your response.
If you can hook both types of readers (and maybe even CONVERT those Skimmers to
Linear readers)… then you really have a winner.
Secret #12: Web Copy Is Copy
Here is something a lot of people don’t know… and that’s that Glazer Kennedy
sells a small fortune of products through its web site every single month. The
number really is pretty staggering.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 19 --

Most people know me for my direct mail offline copy.
However, I have seen enough sales come through our web site to know that there
is HUGE money online… so I have paid attention to online copy very carefully.
This is what I can tell you… there are differences when you write copy for the
web. And you need to understand these critical differences.
HOWEVER, good copy is good copy. Once you master the formula for writing
copy that sells… then you have the skills you need to write killer online copy.
The only thing you need to do is adjust a few specific mechanics of your copy,
and you have instantly mastered online copywriting.
It’s that simple – master the fundamental formula once, and you have the copy
skills to fit any format in existence.
Secret #13: The BIG idea
OK, I will be honest with you – you don’t need a HOOK or a BIG IDEA for
every piece of copy that your write.
HOWEVER, if you want to send your response into the stratosphere… then you
will always look for the big hook.
Up above I told you how I spend much of my time working on the headline. Well,
the hook or Big Idea is the other place where I really focus my time.
You want to come up with the BIG IDEA that sets your product and promotion
apart from everything else.
When you get the BIG IDEA, then the rest of your copy will almost magically fall
in place. This is truly the most exciting thing in copywriting.
In fact, for me… when that BIG IDEA hits I often see the entire sales letter, the
entire story all at once. I still have to work out the mechanics and get the words
down on my computer screen, but it is like a puzzle has been solved.
I don’t have the time or space here to tell you how I go about creating this Big
Idea (and for that, you have my apologies)… but again, this is all part of my
formula.
I sit down, look at a product, I look at the offer… and then I start working on the
BIG IDEA. Often the Big Idea will influence or even change the offer. I will
realize that there is a piece missing, and I will add that into my product or offer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 19 --

HINT: this is usually the FIRST thing that I work on. Before the headline, before
any copy. Sometimes even before the product!
Get the BIG IDEA right, and you can ride it all the way to the bank.
Secret #14: Bullets...
Bullet points sell.
In fact, they sell VERY effectively.
But you need to know how to use them. One of the keys is to make your bullet
points focused on BENEFITS, not on your products features.
Re-read that last sentence, it’s a huge secret that most people overlook. I suppose
it’s understandable… people get so wrapped up in the product they are writing
about, they forget that the important thing is what the product DOES for people.
So they end up writing bullet points that sound like they should be in some
wholesaler’s catalog. But that isn’t copy that sells.
The bullets need to be about the prospective buyer, and each one needs to talk
about a specific benefit that the product will give that prospect.
It’s also a VERY GOOD THING if those bullet points tie into the Big Idea (see
Secret #13).
And one final thing – some people think that you can either have bullets, or you
can have story-based copy. Well, those people are dead wrong. You can easily do
both in your copy. And if you study good copywriting, you will see plenty of
examples.
Secret #15: Email Copy Is Copy
This one is simple – it’s just like Secret #12. In fact, you can just take Secret #12
and substitute the word “email” for the word “web” and we would be all done.
This is the bottom line… good copy is good copy. Whether it shows up in your
mail box, on your web browser, on in your email program.
Sure, there are some formatting differences, but the underlying techniques are all
the same.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 19 --

Once you get the basics of copywriting, then you can write copy in any format
that you want.
Secret #16: Your Market Really IS Different
Lots of time people worry about whether “my style” of copy will work in their
market. Up in Secret #9 I told you that it WOULD work in your market.
Hopefully you take that Secret #9 to heart… because if you do, well then you will
be able to take it to the bank.
However, there is something else I need to mention, and this is another secret that
you won’t hear from those “Copywriting Mystic-Gurus”…
You have an inherent advantage in your market, simply because you are
more familiar with it.
So if you take your knowledge of your specific market and your specific
prospects, and you learn to create good copy (it doesn’t have to be GREAT
copy… even good copy will completely CRUSH your competition), then you
have the recipe for big-time copywriting success.
You don’t need that super-human copy that the Mystic-Gurus keep rubbing in
your face (and shoving down your throat).
If you have copy that sells and you mix it with knowledge of your market, then
you have a winning combination that you can literally take to the bank.
Secret #17: Presenting price
This is something that so many people mess up… well, it just has to be on my list
of secrets.
The big problem for most people is that they get to the end of the sales letter, and
they don’t know what to do with the price of their offer… so they don’t do
anything, they just throw the price out there and HOPE for the best.
This is a big-time mistake.
First, there is a reason the price is at the bottom of the sales letter… because you
can’t present the price until you have properly built the value of your offer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 19 --

There is a reason that McDonald’s has big signs in their window that tells about
their 99 cent special, but there are no signs in the window at the Mercedes dealer
giving the price of their cars.
At 99 cents for a double-cheeseburger, McDonald’s doesn’t have to do much to
build the value of the sale. And they are existing at the very bottom of the food
niche when it comes to pricing power.
Would you rather your business have the pricing power of McDonald’s or the
pricing power of Mercedes?
You have to build value in your sales letter, and after you do that… then you
reveal the price.
Of course, there is a lot more to this subject than I can go into in this report, but
take it from me… you need to have a plan for how you are going to present price,
and you need to have a structure for how you do this.
Again, this is NOT rocket science… but you can’t ignore it either.
You build value in your sales copy, and then you reveal your price.
Secret #18: Stand on the shoulders of giants
This is the one secret that I learned from Dan Kennedy that made everything else
pale in comparison.
Simply put, you don’t need to re-invent the wheel.
You don’t need to start from scratch every time you sit down to write sales copy.
If you were going to build a telescope, then you would study the telescopes of the
past. You would probably look to some experts that could teach you the lessons
they have learned over the years about building telescopes.
Copy is no different. It’s been around for a long time, and lots of brilliant people
have spent a lot of time testing, tweaking and perfecting their copy.
This is an immense resource for anyone who wants to write effective copy. If you
want copy that sells, don’t try to create something from scratch… look at the copy
that has worked in the past.
Use that as a starting point and a model for your copy… and you will be on your
way to creating copy that sells.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 19 --

Secret #19: Get a mentor.
OK, this last secret might be the toughest one to actually follow through on…
which is why I left it for last.
Nevertheless, if you can pull this off, then you are on the short path to
copywriting success.
If you can find a mentor, someone that can show you the ropes in your
copywriting, well… that’s the ticket.
As I mentioned above, you DON’T want to re-invent the wheel. You want to be
able to leverage all the great copywriting talent and skills that have come before
you. The easiest way is to learn from someone else who has been there and
already made the mistakes and figured the important lessons out.
That being said, it can be difficult to find someone who will show the ropes, give
you the short cuts, and help you avoid the mistakes. You want someone who has
been down that road and already MADE THOSE MISTAKES FOR YOU.
Make More Money, More Often, With Less Effort
I have a feeling I might have lost a few friends with this report. I hope not… you
see, most of those “mystic-guru” copywriters are actually pretty nice guys, and
many of them are friends of mine. I think they just tend to get caught up in their
image. Maybe they start believing their own PR.
Hopefully, they understand where I am coming from with this report and I won’t
lose too many friends… it’s just that after I saw all those questions that people
wrote into my survey, well I just had to get this material off my chest.
Obviously, this report is more an “expose” than an end-to-end guide. My goal was
to open your eyes and clear up a lot of the misconceptions about copywriting.
In the coming days I will be releasing more information about my Copy That
Sells online course (it was the responses to my final product survey for that course
that prompted this report).
This course will be unlike anything else that has been seen in the world of
copywriting training… it is structured like a true online university course, with
lessons, video, handouts, and even tests. It takes my entire copywriting formula
and systemizes it into discrete, easy-to-digest steps that are arranged in a step-by-
step fashion.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
